text:
Currently my portfolio is built something like this:

$CRWV ~25%
$HOOD ~24%
$SOFI ~20%
$LMND ~13%
$TEM ~11%

Drastically underperforming YTD, made many mistakes this year. Being heavily concentrated into $HIMS earlier in the year and selling around ~$19 for a big loss. Was into $DUOL earlier in the year and sold for a loss. Being underexposed to $NBIS when it was in the ~$70 range which I sold the last of my position around ~$192 and rotated into $CRWV. Being heavy into fintech which has been having a rough year. No exposure to Memory/photonics theme.

Investing and managing my portfolio is the most humbling thing I’ve ever done, I love it. Looking forward to continuing this journey and learning along the way!

• Critical Minerals: $MP, $UUUU

Find the theme.

Find the leaders.

Stay with them longer than everyone else.

text:
Bir başka diğer bahsetmek istediğim şirket ise ;

$COHR 

Anlık Fiyat :389 #dolar

COHR Tam Olarak Ne İş Yapıyor?

Coherent Corp.

Coherent, dünyanın en büyük fotonik (ışık teknolojileri) şirketlerinden biridir.

Basit anlatırsak:

NVDA hesap yapıyor.✅

 MU veriyi depoluyor.✅

 COHR ise bu verinin veri merkezinde ışıkla taşınmasını sağlıyor. ✅

Yani yapay zekanın sinir sistemi diyebileceğimiz katmanda yer alıyor. (İşte en can alıcı nokta bu )

Şirketin Ana İş Kolları : 

1. Optik Transceiver'lar

Bunlar veri merkezlerinde kullanılan özel modüller.

Görevleri:

GPU → GPU
Sunucu → Sunucu
Rack → Rack

arasındaki veriyi ışıkla taşımak.

AI modelleri büyüdükçe bu ürünlerin önemi katlanıyor. ⚡️⚡️

2. Silicon Photonics

Bence şirketin en kritik alanı.

Elektrik sinyallerini ışığa dönüştürüyor.

Avantajları:

Daha az enerji tüketimi✅

Daha yüksek hız✅

Daha düşük gecikme✅

Daha az ısı✅

NVIDIA'nın gelecek nesil veri merkezi mimarisinde tam olarak ihtiyaç duyulan teknoloji bu.

3. Lazerler

COHR dünyanın en büyük endüstriyel ve telekom lazer üreticilerinden biri.

Ürettikleri:

InP (Indium Phosphide) lazerler
CW lazerler
VCSEL lazerler
Telekom lazerleri

Bu lazerler hem kendi ürünlerinde hem de sektörün diğer oyuncularında kullanılıyor.

4. Co-Packaged Optics (CPO)

Şu anda Wall Street'in en heyecanlandığı konu.

Bugün:

GPU ↔ Optik modül ayrı.

Yarın:

GPU ↔ Optik modül aynı pakette olacak.

Bu teknolojiye CPO deniyor.

NVIDIA'nın gelecekteki veri merkezi planlarının merkezinde bu teknoloji var. 

COHR da burada ana tedarikçilerden biri olmaya çalışıyor.

Kimlerle Çalışıyor?

Kesin müşteri listeleri her zaman açıklanmıyor ancak sektör analizlerinde ve şirket sunumlarında şu isimler sürekli karşımıza çıkıyor:

NVIDIA
Microsoft Azure
Amazon Web Services
Meta
Google Cloud

Çünkü bunların hepsi devasa AI veri merkezleri kuruyor ve optik ağ altyapısına ihtiyaç duyuyorlar.

NVIDIA ile İlişkisi

Burası çok önemli.

2026'da $NVDA :

Coherent'e yaklaşık 2 milyar dolar yatırım yaptı.

Uzun vadeli satın alma anlaşmaları yaptı.

Lazer ve optik bileşen kapasitesini garanti altına aldı.

Bu haber çıktığında COHR hissesi sert yükselmişti.

Çünkü bu durum:

"NVIDIA gelecekte fotoniğin kritik olacağını düşünüyor."

mesajını verdi.

Neden Yapay Zeka İçin Kritik?

Bugün darboğaz:

GPU sayısı değil.

GPU'ların birbirleriyle haberleşmesi.

Rakipleri Kimler?

En büyük rakipler:

Lumentum Holdings $LITE 

Applied Optoelectronics $AAOI 

Corning $GLW 

Broadcom $AVGO 

Marvell Technology $MRVL

Ama COHR'nin farkı:

Lazer + Transceiver + Silicon Photonics + CPO bileşenlerini aynı çatı altında sunabilmesi.

text:
$MRVL 

ANLIK FİYAT : 310 #DOLAR

Sektör: Yarı İletkenler (Semiconductors) / Yapay Zeka Altyapısı

Ne iş yapıyor?

Yapay zeka veri merkezleri için özel tasarım çipler (ASIC/XPU) geliştiriyor.

Veri merkezlerindeki sunucuların birbirleriyle konuşmasını sağlayan networking (ağ) çipleri ve switch'ler üretiyor.

AI kümelerinde kullanılan optik bağlantı (silicon photonics, DSP) çözümleri sunuyor.

Depolama ve yüksek hızlı veri aktarım çipleri geliştiriyor.

AI dünyasındaki yeri nedir?

$NVDA 'nın yaptığı gibi genel amaçlı GPU satmıyor.
Daha çok "AI veri merkezlerinin sinir sistemi" tarafında çalışıyor.

Ayrıca Amazon, Microsoft gibi devlere özel AI çipleri tasarlıyor. 

Bu nedenle çoğu yatırımcı $MRVL'yi, AI altyapısında Broadcom $AVGO  ile aynı kategoride değerlendiriyor.

Tek cümlelik özet:

MRVL, yapay zeka veri merkezlerinin birbirine bağlanmasını sağlayan ağ ve optik çipler üreten, aynı zamanda bulut devlerine özel AI çipleri tasarlayan bir yarı iletken şirketi..

text:
This week I learned the photonics trade is finally shifting from vibes to contracts.

$AXTI got a RMB173M / USD25.4M InP wafer deal with prepay/minimum-buy language. IQE got Tower commitments. AEHR got a follow-on SiPh test order.

Still tiny-scout territory. Proof is not size.
Go $GLW guys if you want to participate in the Photonics scale-up 😉

if you run some simple estimations, you'll find regardless of whatever optical engines you use, the scale-up among multiple racks will need thousands to tens of thousands of optical fibers running in and out of each rack.

text:
The 5 best stocks and ETFs to own right now: 👇

$DRAM - memory ETF that includes $SNDK, $MU, Samsung and more. This is a growing space and will only continue to grow.

$TSLA - high and volatile gas prices are increasing demand for EVs. Energy storage systems, charging stations and autonomous capabilities are growing immensely. Robots are coming soon.

$SOFI - with oil stabilizing over time, the chances for rate cuts due to lower inflation is higher. This company is highly profitable and is scaling up quickly. They’re projecting 40% growth over the next 3-4 years.

$AAOI - photonics is a huge market and is growing in demand as AI data centers scale. These guys are positioned well to crush it over the next 5+ years. 

$ONDS - there has been a huge increase in the demand of autonomous drones, boats and on land vehicles used in military operations across the globe. Ondas has all the right products and services the U.S. military and other allied nations would need. 

What 5 stocks would you choose? 🤔

Yet its revenue model remains heavily linked to finished transceiver units. When thinking about their insulation to oversupply... AAOI offers hyperscalers: > A U.S.-anchored supply chain > Internal laser technology > Automated manufacturing > Large planned 800G and 1.6T capacity > TAA-compatible product options > A hedge against China concentration > CPO and ELSFP optionality They say that U.S. production is attractive to customers and that automation limits the cost penalty associated with domestic manufacturing. Demand currently exceeds planned capacity through mid-2027. Even with the massive capacity ramp. AOI also has several concentrated risks: > Finished-module ASP exposure > Customer concentration > Large manufacturing ramp > Yield execution > Final-test capacity > Component availability > Working-capital demands > Forecast-demand conversion The planned move from 100,000 monthly units to more than 650,000 in less than a year is operationally demanding. A capacity ramp of that size can create inefficiency even when demand remains strong. text:
"AI data centers hit a new bottleneck, optical interconnects"

When their hypes are over, now their reality in AI chip supply chain. 

I don't need to be charted, if I was selected at my start.

Your investors should know : Manufacturing in testing layers means where optical interconnect scaling and silicon photonics capacity are emerging as key constraints on AI data centre buildout. 

Which aligns with this week's observation, where AI data centres handles the scaling in larger workloads, in sell-offs, would they trigger the physical infrastructure connecting chips inside those facilities has becoming a limiting factor. 

Don't stir round their bs, this is fact. 

"Optical interconnects, which use light rather than electrical signals to move data, increased to be seen as the solution to bandwidth and power constraints at scale."

You tell me how this shift towards? 
It's not a design challenge, but more of "manufacturing and testing challenge", such as equipment suppliers, test solution providers, contract manufacturers, all play roles in determining how quickly optical interconnects technology can react high-volume production.

You tested that before, I know, how's the sudden pull-off, as if capacity running out, did you find remedies, when the heat is up, more volumes to scale increases of demands creation for specialised equipment and test infrastructure across several companies, typically component availability could associate as near-term execution risks. New partnerships and equipment order announcements signal that the photonics manufacturing ecosystem is highly depend on, actively expanding t meet AI data centre demand. 

Revenue targeting, your thought on these, AI chip supply chain in focus : semiconductor equipment, process control, foundry services, specialty chip design, all these companies in your industry.

Metrics: 
- CapEx, < 80%, over 100%, high risk 
- Free cash flow, keep slipping, nope. 
- Cloud services revenue and profitability, matched? 
- Management's perception : continual and rising demands, now changed into the tone of "Capital efficiency" to "slow the tempo down". 

A whole rinse up, bubbles to all their fluffs. 
The rest that can be existed is actually running with the infrastructure, users centric, enterprise architecture, may I remind you? 

If their pimps are still sitting at RFPs levels, they can't let you know how I wrote here, what makes you think I looked at those since 2019? :D

That's why 2024 dropped, 2025, unbothered and pushed harder, now what, you want to shut who up?

For sure, shut yours up next if they don't know what "AI adoption" means! 

What compliance project are you sourcing out? 
What's your market like now? :D

text:
💡Montana is well-equipped with a supply chain for a 21st century economy💡

In collaboration with the @MontanaChamber, our Policy Director @TannerMAvery recently gave a presentation on what data centers mean for Montana.🖥️ Highlighting that Montana has abundant critical minerals, high-tech photonics, and chip-manufacturing, Tanner shares: “when I think about data centers, I don’t just think that we should attract data centers for the sake of data centers. I’m thinking that when we can attract parts of the supply chain, we can attract the entire supply chain.”

text:
$XFAB upgraded to Outperform with a €12.8 PT by Bernstein — up from €5.

Bullish call. But I think it's still undershooting the real optionality here.

🧵

The obvious thesis:

Automotive recovery + SiC/power semi ramp. Classic reactive upgrade. Bernstein is pricing in the cycle, not the platform.

What the market isn't pricing in yet:

$XFAB is quietly building a silicon photonics foundry capability through PhotonixFAB — in partnership with NVIDIA and LIGENTEC for thin-film lithium niobate (TFLN) on SOI.

If that platform matures into HVM (high-volume manufacturing), the valuation conversation changes completely.

You're not talking about a specialty analog/mixed-signal foundry anymore.

You're talking about a photonics-capable specialty foundry at the intersection of AI infrastructure, optical interconnects, and next-gen sensing.

That's a different multiple entirely.

My take:
Bernstein's upgrade is right on direction, wrong on magnitude.

The PT reflects the cyclical recovery. It doesn't reflect what XFAB becomes if the photonics bet hits.

Still bullish. Watching closely.

$XFAB

text:
Bernstein just upgraded X-FAB to Outperform with a price target of €12.80. While the upgrade is a welcome acknowledgment, I believe it’s still fundamentally reactive—primarily focused on automotive recovery and the SiC/power semiconductor ramp-up.

​The real upside? The market has yet to fully price in their potential move to High-Volume Manufacturing $HVM. With their work in silicon photonics (PhotonixFAB/Nvidia) and TFLN on SOI (LIGENTEC), X-FAB is positioned for much more than just a cyclical recovery. 

Bullish. $XFAb

text:
Aeluma $ALMU just made one of the most interesting hires I've seen from the company.

Brendan Moran spent 18 years at Lumileds helping take photonics products from R&D into high-volume manufacturing.
25+ products launched.

4+ billion units shipped.

Managed a mobile portfolio exceeding $300M in annual revenue.

This is not a scientist hire.
This is a commercialization hire.

Watch what companies DO, not what they SAY.

#Semiconductors #Photonics #SiliconPhotonics #ALMU

text:
Nvidia spent $6.5B on photonics in 8 weeks. Copper can't keep up with 100K-GPU clusters.

Feynman (2028) drops copper entirely - NVLink 8 runs on fiber. TSMC's CPO is in high-volume production. Besi: 10x power, 20x latency gains.

Here's the part nobody talks about: AI data centers need 36x more fiber than traditional racks. Cable lead times hit one year. Preform expansion takes 18-24 months.

The next AI bottleneck isn't chip supply. It's glass.

#SiliconPhotonics #Nvidia #AISupplyChain #CPO

text:
Portfolio update:

Today was one of those days where doing nothing was the trade.

No covered calls.
No CSPs.
No chasing.
No trimming.
Just let the AI infra book work.
Daily P&L: +2.74%
Open P&L: +87.26%
Cash: 21.17%
Current allocation:
$NBIS — 27.11%
Largest position, red today but still up big overall. Core neocloud position.
$MU — 21.71%
Monster day into earnings week. Anthropic partnership added even more confirmation to the memory thesis.

Cash — 21.17%
Still keeping dry powder. Cash is a weapon, not a drag.
$MRVL — 8.07%
Laggard today, but still a core AI interconnect/custom silicon position.
$CRDO — 6.79%
Huge day. Connectivity trade is alive and well.

$DRAM — 6.27%
Memory basket continues to work with $MU.

$VRT — 4.28%
Power/cooling still confirming the AI data center buildout.
$COHR — 3.36%
Photonics/laser chokepoint had a monster move.

$ALAB — 1.31%
Still too small. I want more over time, but not chasing vertical candles.
Big lesson today:

The clean book won.
No options capping the winners.
No obligations forcing decisions.
No overtrading.

Sometimes the most professional move is sitting on your hands while the thesis plays out.

text:
Here $SIVE explains that autonomous systems (self-driving vehicles, robots or EdgeAI) need two essential things to work well

One of its many areas of expertise, as we’ve already mentioned isn’t only the photonics

Precise detection:

Thanks to its FMCW LiDAR technology, which uses high-power DFB lasers

This solution measures both distance and object speed with great accuracy

Even when they are moving, while keeping energy consumption low

Reliable connectivity:

Its high frequency wireless solutions (mmWave) enable fast

Low latency communication between devices essential for making real time decisions

$SIVE offers both the “eyes” (advanced sensing) and the “voice” (robust connectivity) that make safe and efficient autonomy possible in the future

text:
Me canso, no te lo repito más 🎯

SOITEC $SOI.PA controla prácticamente el cuello de botella de una tecnología clave para los smartphones, los autos eléctricos y ahora… la inteligencia artificial ‼️
Es el líder mundial indiscutido en obleas de Silicon-on-Insulator (SOI). Su tecnología patentada Smart Cut, desarrollada junto con el CEA-Leti, es una especie de ‘escalpelo atómico’: permite transferir una capa ultrafina de silicio cristalino perfecto sobre un aislante. Eso reduce fugas de energía, mejora el rendimiento y baja el consumo.
Gracias a esto, Soitec domina el mercado de sustratos avanzados. 

‼️En Photonics-SOI —el material base para los transceptores ópticos que están revolucionando los data centers de IA— está cerca del monopolio. Prácticamente todas las grandes fundiciones que fabrican chips fotónicos para IA dependen de sus obleas.
En el móvil, sus obleas RF-SOI están dentro de casi todos los smartphones premium del mundo, mejorando la señal 5G y el consumo de batería. En automoción, sus soluciones Power-SOI y FD-SOI ayudan a que los vehículos eléctricos y los sistemas ADAS sean más eficientes.
La compañía facturó cerca de 600 millones de euros en su último ejercicio fiscal, tuvo un año complicado por corrección de inventarios en móviles, pero su negocio de Photonics-SOI para IA ya superó los 100 millones de dólares y sigue creciendo fuerte.
Con más de 2.100 empleados, plantas en Francia y Singapur, y más de 4.300 patentes, Soitec es uno de esos jugadores invisibles pero críticos de la cadena de suministro de semiconductores. Sin ellos, muchos de los avances en eficiencia energética y en la explosión de la IA simplemente serían mucho más caros o difíciles de escalar.
Una joya tecnológica francesa que vale la pena seguir de cerca. 🇫🇷🔬

text:
🚨 Jensen Huang in SHERMAN, TX?!? 🚨

For those of you asking, "Who in the world is Jensen Huang?" ... keep reading.

What was once a quiet North Texas town is quickly becoming a major semiconductor and technology hub. We're not talking about AI chatbots writing emails or generating funny pictures. We're talking about the technology that makes AI possible in the first place.

That's a big reason why Jensen Huang visited Sherman. As AI demand explodes, the need for high-speed optical networking and photonics is growing right alongside it.

If you've never heard of him, he's the CEO of NVIDIA. The company at the center of the AI revolution and one of the most valuable companies on the planet.

NVIDIA has committed to producing up to $500 billion in AI infrastructure in the United States, including significant investments right here in Texas.

Last week, Coherent broke ground on an expanded Sherman facility that will help power the next generation of AI infrastructure. The company manufactures critical components used to move massive amounts of data at incredible speeds, exactly what AI systems require.

Coherent also announced a $50 million CHIPS Act grant, adding to previous state and local investments supporting the project.

Think about that for a minute...

✅ Billions in investment
✅ Thousands of jobs
✅ Major infrastructure projects
✅ Global technology companies betting on Sherman

This isn't just growth.

It's transformation.

📍 Keep your eye on Sherman. The rest of the country already is.

Ten years from now, people may look back and realize Sherman wasn't just growing, it was quietly becoming one of the biggest economic opportunities in North Texas.

#shermantx #NorthTexasGrowth #artificialintelligence #graysoncounty #dfwrealestate #northtexasrealestate Image credit: @nvidia

text:
Many people ask why I am aggressively positioned across this specific supply chain: $SIVE, $AAOI, ShunSin, and FOCI.

​The biggest bottleneck in AI is no longer software, it’s physics. For the next generation of compute to function, datacenters must solve a critical equation:

1-Consume less power.

2-Generate less heat.

3- Multiply data bandwidth.

​Copper cables have hit their physical limits (too slow, too hot, too power-hungry). The only absolute structural solution is CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) and Silicon Photonics: replacing electricity with light.

​Here is why this exact basket of stocks is the literal chokepoint of this revolution:

​🔹 $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) - The Engine:

To have optics, you need a light source. Sivers manufactures the DFB laser arrays. Their specific architecture is the only one capable of providing the necessary power to hyperscalers while massively reducing the thermal footprint. It's the ignition.

​🔹 FOCI - The Optical Bridge:

Generating light is useless if you can't route it with surgical precision. FOCI is the leader in fiber arrays and micro-optic coupling. They connect this light to the silicon with zero signal loss.

​🔹 ShunSin - The Architect (Advanced Packaging):

Integrating lasers and fibers directly onto a chip requires atomic-level assembly technology (OSAT). ShunSin holds the advanced packaging expertise to assemble all these optical components as close to the AI processor as physically possible.

​🔹 $AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) - The Deployment:

Once the chips are equipped, millions of servers need to be connected. AAOI mass-produces the ultra-high-speed optical transceivers and network infrastructure so the entire datacenter can communicate at the speed of light.

​While the retail herd chases chip designers at absurd valuations, smart money is quietly cornering the hardware bottleneck that is physically required for AI to scale by 2027.

​I don't trade hope. I trade physics. 🐺💡

#SiliconPhotonics #AI #CPO #Tech

text:
Identify the asymmetry in the rack and you'll find it in the stocks. 

Remember the insight that set off the memory supercycle? HBM per GPU went from 80GB on Hopper to a full TERABYTE on Rubin Ultra. 

Or how about when $INTC's CEO said the ratio of CPUs to GPUs would 8x?

Well the next asymmetric content step up in the data center is power chips.

NVIDIA's 2027 rack pulls 600,000 watts. That's 30x a normal server rack. At today's voltage the copper would melt. The only fix is going from 48V to 800V, and that forces every power chip in the rack to change.

Our modeling on power chip content per NVIDIA rack, Hopper to Rubin Ultra:

Power supply: $1,512 → $26,400 (17x)
GPU power board: $1,728 → $16,000 (9x)
Voltage regulators: $1,700 → $8,000 (5x)

GPUs had their run. CPUs had their run. Memory had its run. Photonics had its run. 

Power semis are just getting started. 

Just dropped the full breakdown on power semis: the physics explained for dummies, the three places inside the rack where the money goes, and the 15 name basket ranked by conviction. Link below or at bio.

text:
While everyone was talking about AI and photonics I talked about gays.
$GRND is up almost 30% since I mentioned it.
Gays don't like football but they are surely playing with balls during the FIFA world cup. ⚽️
And what do they need to find more balls? Grindr.
Now the question is, can Grindr stock breakout and reach new highs?
embedded_text:
I'm not gay but $GRND is getting kinda interesting here 🧐
18 ntm p/e while growing revenues 20% at 30%+ EBIT margin.
I feel like this could have a stronger moat than $MTCH because gay people probably need a dating app more than normal people 🌈
No position (no pun intended 😉) https://t.co/sAdd9zC6m5

text:
A lot of the AI infrastructure thesis from the past few weeks are starting to play out.

Neocloud / Sovereign AI:
$CRWV 100 → 121
$NBIS 228 → 297

Memory:
Still holding dram long from ~68, added more $SKHYNIX
$MU earnings next week could be another important read-through for HBM demand. (Jun 24)

Photonics:

China just approved the first major 2026 InP substrate export batch, providing some near-term relief to one of the optical bottlenecks discussed in my photonics thread.

I’m still watching this closely. Demand for 800G/1.6T AI interconnects continues to explode, so a single export batch doesn’t solve the structural shortage, it simply unlocks some capacity. The real question is whether supply can keep up with AI cluster buildouts over the next 12–24 months.

Meanwhile:
• HBM4E sampling next month
• SK Hynix US ADR listing
• SK Hynix earnings (Jul 23)
• Ongoing sovereign AI buildouts globally

The AI trade keeps moving up the stack.

First GPUs.
Then neoclouds.
Then photonics.
Now memory.

The biggest winners continue to come from identifying the bottlenecks before the market fully prices them in.

text:
🇺🇸 | نتائج توصيات السوق الأمريكي اليوم

يوم جديد يؤكد أن قوة التداول ليست في كثرة الصفقات، بل في دقة الاختيار وإدارة الخروج.

تم اليوم إغلاق صفقتين على أرباح قوية:

✅سهم Laser Photonics LASE
من 2.38 إلى 2.65
ربح محقق: +11.3%

✅سهم Roblox RBLX
من 43 إلى 49.13
ربح محقق: +14.3%

وفي المقابل، تم إغلاق صفقة ResMed RMD عند وقف الخسارة بخسارة محدودة تقارب -2%، وهذا جزء طبيعي من أي خطة تداول منضبطة.

📍الخلاصة:
نترك الأرباح تكبر عندما تسير الصفقة في صالحنا، ونغلق الخسارة مبكرًا عندما لا تؤكد الحركة توقعاتنا.

💥هكذا تُبنى النتائج:
ربح قوي عند تحقق الأهداف، وخسارة محدودة عند فشل السيناريو.

 📍في Stock Basket، نعتمد على الدمج بين إدارة الصفقة باحتراف، والبوت الذكي الذي يساعدنا في فرز وتصنيف الفرص داخل السوق قبل التنفيذ.

👈 تفضل واحجز مكانك لمتابعة صفقات واضحة بمستويات دخول، أهداف، حماية ربح، وإدارة دقيقة أثناء الجلسة، 
-الانضمام للمجموعة الذهبية هو الخطوة الأقرب لك.

📩 للاشتراك والتفاصيل، راسلنا الآن.

⚠️ إخلاء مسؤولية:
جميع المعلومات لأغراض إعلامية فقط وليست توصية مباشرة. يُرجى الرجوع إلى مستشار مالي مرخّص قبل أي قرار.

text:
Not claiming $SIVE builds the plasmonic modulator itself.

The point is that plasmonics does not remove the need for high-performance laser sources it makes the optical input layer even more critical.

That is exactly where Sivers is positioned: high-power DFB laser arrays, external light sources and silicon-photonics integration for AI datacenters / CPO.

And this is now supported by the GlobalFoundries $GFS collaboration, where Sivers’ laser arrays are being integrated into reference designs on GF’s silicon photonics platform, including SCALE for next-gen optical sub-assemblies.

So yes from a system architecture perspective, this is highly relevant to $SIVE

text:
It used to take a carefully controlled lab setup to shape light with precision. Now, that same level of control is moving into real-world systems. 

Spatial light modulators, or SLMs, are evolving to be faster, more stable, and ready for deployment—helping engineers fine-tune light for everything from advanced imaging to quantum applications. 

Hamamatsu’s Brandon Gorakhnauth shares his insights on how what was once experimental is becoming essential and where photonics is headed next. 🔗 https://t.co/jdRyuOvn4e 

#Photonics #Optics #SLM #Engineering #Innovation

text:
Contemporary silicon photonics modulators are constrained by the diffraction limit of light, restricting their ability to scale efficiently to the bandwidths AI infrastructure will require. Plasmonics offers a path forward.

By compressing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface, plasmonic modulators can operate at speeds exceeding 1 THz, more than 10x faster than conventional photonic modulators, while measuring approximately 10 microns in length, 300x to 500x shorter. The result is exponentially higher bandwidth density within the same silicon photonics platform, without requiring a shift to new material systems.

Marvell Senior Director Claudia Hoessbacher and Director Wolfgang Heni of Optical Engineering detail the technology, its applications across scale-up and scale-across networks, and where development stands today.

Read more: https://t.co/3z9yPJxzac

Plasmonics offers a path forward. By compressing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface, plasmonic modulators can operate at speeds exceeding 1 THz, more than 10x faster than conventional photonic modulators, while measuring approximately 10 microns in length, 300x to 500x shorter. The result is exponentially higher bandwidth density within the same silicon photonics platform, without requiring a shift to new material systems. Marvell Senior Director Claudia Hoessbacher and Director Wolfgang Heni of Optical Engineering detail the technology, its applications across scale-up and scale-across networks, and where development stands today. 🇪🇸 EL EFECTO TÚNEL EN LA ESCALA PICO: ASÍ SE ENCIENDEN LAS ESTRELLAS 🌟⚛️

"Al igual que el efecto túnel permite a las estrellas encenderse a una temperatura muy por debajo del umbral de fusión clásica... es el mismo principio que rige la transferencia de fotones individuales". Con esta genialidad, la Dra. Eviana Alice Breuss (Consejo Académico de IMIX) decodifica la física estelar profunda. Desde la Filosofía de Sistemas Complejos, no observamos la materia de forma pasiva; descodificamos las oscilaciones de plasma y la fotónica cuántica moderna en la escala picométrica para diseñar la infraestructura energética del mañana. Bajo la dirección del Dr. Federico Vidal, IMIX transforma esta ciencia de frontera en una arquitectura proactiva, unificándonos como un sistema de múltiples mentes para acelerar la maestría energética sustentable, mitigar el Gran Filtro y consolidar la Transición hacia una Civilización Tipo 1.

--->

🇺🇸 THE TUNNEL EFFECT AT THE PICO SCALE: HOW STARS IGNITE 🌟⚛️

"Just as electron tunneling allows stars to ignite at a temperature far below the classical fusion threshold... it is the exact same principle that governs the transfer of individual photons." With this genius insight, Dr. Eviana Alice Breuss (IMIX Academic Council) decodes profound stellar physics. Through Complex Systems Philosophy, we do not passively observe matter; we decode plasma oscillations and modern quantum photonics at the picometric scale to engineer tomorrow's energetic infrastructure. Under the leadership of Dr. Federico Vidal, IMIX transforms this frontier science into a proactive architecture, unifying us as a multi-minded system to accelerate sustainable energetic mastery, mitigate the Great Filter, and consolidate the Transition toward a Type 1 Civilization.

VIGO PHOTONICS SPÓŁKA AKCYJNA (WIG80-#VGO)----19:57----

VIGO Photonics planuje emisję do 131.219 akcji serii G i możliwą sprzedaż do 124.800 akcji przez głównego akcjonariusza

Potencjalny wpływ: Istotny

This is one of those announcements that’s easy to overlook but could become much bigger if it plays out.

NUBURU isn’t just talking about lasers anymore. It’s positioning its blue-laser technology for underwater power infrastructure through the DEEP LIGHT project, which is backed by the Italian Navy. That’s an interesting combination of photonics, defense, and critical infrastructure.

The key thing to remember is that this is still subject to final agreements and technical validation, so it’s not guaranteed revenue yet. But if the project moves into commercialization, it could open the door to a much larger market than many investors are currently pricing in.

Definitely one to keep on the watchlist. $BURU

We are particularly honored by the involvement of General Pietro Serino, former Chief of Staff of the Italian Army, whose distinguished military career spans decades of leadership, strategic planning, international defense cooperation, and operational command at the highest levels.

General Serino’s experience and insight bring significant value as we continue advancing our defense and security strategy, including the planned acquisition of Tekne and the expansion of our broader ecosystem across defense mobility, electronic warfare, critical infrastructure protection, operational resilience, and next-generation directed-energy technologies.

The participation of leaders with proven military and defense expertise reinforces our belief that successful execution requires not only innovative technologies, but also strategic guidance from individuals who understand the evolving needs of modern defense organizations.

As we continue executing our vision, we remain focused on building a platform positioned to support innovation, strengthen strategic partnerships, and create sustainable long-term value for our shareholders.

We thank our shareholders for their continued support and confidence as we advance this important next chapter.

text:
That Jabil print is a massive wake-up call for anyone who thinks AI infrastructure spending is cooling off.

While the broader market has been sweating over whether hyperscale capex is going to slow down, Jabil’s Q3 numbers and raised guidance prove that the physical buildout is actually accelerating. When a manufacturing giant that handles the servers, networking, and data center assembly for the biggest players in the world lifts its outlook, it’s a direct proxy for what's coming downstream.
Here is the reality for the optical and photonics space:

The Capex Slowdown Is a Myth: Jabil didn't just beat earnings; they raised their Q4 revenue guidance to a massive $9.2B–$10.0B. CEO Mike Dastoor explicitly stated that AI infrastructure demand is "extremely strong" and pushed their full-year AI revenue outlook meaningfully higher. The physical hardware layer is getting plugged in as fast as they can build it.

The Institutional Trigger for $SIVE: Pushing past the 100 SEK ($10) mark is a psychological and structural milestone. For a lot of institutional funds, crossing that threshold clears the internal liquidity mandates that previously kept them on the sidelines. It opens up a completely new class of buyer for the stock.

Direct Read-Through for $AAOI : Jabil's raised outlook signals a massive volume ramp in networking components. You cannot scale these next-gen AI clusters without an enormous amount of optical transceivers and advanced routing hardware, which directly backstops the demand runway for independent component suppliers.

The market generalists are still debating macro sentiment, but the actual corporate data shows the physical backbone of AI is hitting maximum utilization.
Still hyper-bullish the optical super-cycle. 

Long $SIVE and $AAOI.

People don't even know who will be picked or dropped by bigger firms but expect to make money on all of them.

I hope you'll know when to exit as well.

Way too speculative to put actual money behind.

Discover what this means for quantum routing, fidelity, and scalability. Details below.

text:
$NOK 

Nokia's aggressive shift toward AI infrastructure shares an extensive history of building high-performance computing platforms together. Furthermore, a critical operational overlap links Nokia's hardware architecture directly to the specialized semiconductor developer Sivers Semiconductors

leadership layer is composed of deeply interconnected tech executives, many of whom came to Nokia directly from Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE): 
•Justin Hotard (President & CEO): An expert in supercomputing. Before joining Nokia in 2025, he was the Executive Vice President & GM of Intel's Data Center & AI Group. Prior to Intel, he spent years at HPE running their High-Performance Computing (HPC), AI, and Labs business units. 
•Konstanty Owczarek (Chief Corporate Development Officer): A veteran of tech M&A and corporate strategy. He worked directly beneath Hotard at HPE, serving as the Chief Strategy Officer and COO for the HPC, AI, and Labs business group. 
•Victoria Hanrahan (Chief of Staff to the CEO): Also part of the core HPE cloud group, she served as the Vice President of Global Marketing for HPE’s Artificial Intelligence and High-Performance Compute business. 
•David Heard (President of Network Infrastructure): He joined Nokia via the Infinera acquisition, where he was the CEO. He brings decades of specialized experience in optical networking, data center interconnects, and telecom systems. 

The Sivers Semiconductors Connection
The strategic connection between Nokia and Sivers Semiconductors is anchored by a high-profile technology executive who bridges both companies: 
•Karin Raj: Currently serving as Nokia's CTO for Europe, she was nominated to the Board of Directors for Sivers Semiconductors.
•Sivers Semiconductors specializes in Photonics for AI Data Centers and advanced mmWave chips.
•Having a top Nokia technology executive sitting directly on the Sivers board creates a tight development loop. It ensures Nokia's optical routing division gets direct access to the laser/photonics tech required to handle massive AI data center interconnects.

text:
AI 산업 병목 분야 분석 (2026년 6월 기준)
AI 성장의 병목이 GPU에서 전력, HBM 메모리 대역폭, CPO/광인터커넥트로 이동했습니다.
주요 결론
2026년 현재 AI 인프라 확장의 가장 큰 걸림돌은 칩 자체가 아니라, 전력을 공급하고, 데이터를 빠르게 주고받고, 메모리 대역폭을 확보하는 물리적 인프라입니다. GPU 공급은 어느 정도 풀렸지만, 데이터센터를 실제로 돌리는 데 필요한 인프라가 따라가지 못하고 있습니다.
1. 전력 인프라 (가장 심각한 병목)
•  미국에서 2026년 계획된 데이터센터 capacity의 **30~50%**가 지연 또는 취소될 가능성이 제기됨
•  Transformer lead time이 2~5년까지 늘어남 (예전에는 24개월 수준)
•  Hyperscalers가 수백억 달러를 투자해도 그리드와 전력 장비 공급이 bottleneck
•  해결 방향: On-site generation + SiC/GaN 고효율 전력 반도체 (800V+), 효율적 냉각
2. HBM 메모리 (Memory Bandwidth Wall)
•  GPU compute는 충분해졌으나 메모리 대역폭이 새 병목
•  Micron과 SK Hynix의 2026년 HBM 공급이 이미 완판
•  AI가 전체 메모리 생산의 큰 비중을 차지하면서 consumer DRAM 쪽까지 영향 확산
•  HBM4/HBM4E ramp가 2027년 이후 핵심 변수
3. 인터커넥트 & 광통신 (CPO / Photonics)
•  GPU 클러스터 내부와 랙 간 데이터 이동이 전력과 대역폭의 큰 부분 차지 (네트워킹이 전체 전력의 10% 이상)
•  **CPO(Co-Packaged Optics)**가 전력 효율과 bandwidth density를 동시에 높이는 핵심 솔루션
•  다만 packaging 복잡도, thermal, yield, 표준화, 서비스 용이성 등의 과제가 남아 있음
•  Photonics foundry와 optical networking 기업 역할 확대 중
4. 첨단 패키징 & 공급망
•  Chiplet + CPO + HBM 통합을 위한 advanced packaging (CoWoS 등) capacity가 타이트
•  Transformer/switchgear 등 전력 장비 공급망도 장기 bottleneck
•  TSMC, Intel, Amkor, X-FAB, DB HiTek 등 specialty foundry 움직임 중요

HBM 메모리 (Memory Bandwidth Wall) • GPU compute는 충분해졌으나 메모리 대역폭이 새 병목 • Micron과 SK Hynix의 2026년 HBM 공급이 이미 완판 • AI가 전체 메모리 생산의 큰 비중을 차지하면서 consumer DRAM 쪽까지 영향 확산 • HBM4/HBM4E ramp가 2027년 이후 핵심 변수 3. 인터커넥트 & 광통신 (CPO / Photonics) • GPU 클러스터 내부와 랙 간 데이터 이동이 전력과 대역폭의 큰 부분 차지 (네트워킹이 전체 전력의 10% 이상) • **CPO(Co-Packaged Optics)**가 전력 효율과 bandwidth density를 동시에 높이는 핵심 솔루션 • 다만 packaging 복잡도, thermal, yield, 표준화, 서비스 용이성 등의 과제가 남아 있음 • Photonics foundry와 optical networking 기업 역할 확대 중 4. 첨단 패키징 & 공급망 • Chiplet + CPO + HBM 통합을 위한 advanced packaging (CoWoS 등) capacity가 타이트 • Transformer/switchgear 등 전력 장비 공급망도 장기 bottleneck • TSMC, Intel, Amkor, X-FAB, DB HiTek 등 specialty foundry 움직임 중요"}}}, "rest_id": "2067985350249914575", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "196", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

2 more years mirrors 2000s but with real revenues.

$MU $DRAM $EWY and semi plays and photonics along with energy/GPU plays is where I'm at.
$WULF $IREN $NBIS $CIFR etc
